competitorResearchQueue.name: Rogers Pets
competitorResearchQueue.targetPattern: I got a fictional pet simulation style videos

contentBriefs.title: AI Pet Simulation Content Strategy
contentBriefs.summary: Rogers Pets and Pet Simulated World are leading the AI-generated pet niche. Focus on 'I got a fictional pet' format with simulation elements.
contentBriefs.hook: I just adopted a pet that doesn't exist in real life... and it's growing faster than I expected

contentBriefs.title: AI Creature Design Video
contentBriefs.summary: Creature Lab shows AI creature generation is trending. Document the process of creating unique creatures with AI tools.
contentBriefs.hook: I used AI to create a creature that could never exist... then I brought it to life

contentBriefs.title: Night Toons Style Animation
contentBriefs.summary: Night Toons combines animation with storytelling. Create animated shorts with unexpected twists.
contentBriefs.hook: This 2-minute animation has a twist that changes everything

contentBriefs.title: Sacred Mystery Unboxing
contentBriefs.summary: Sacred Stuff curates mystery content. Create high-stakes unboxing with unknown value items.
contentBriefs.hook: I paid $500 for a mystery box from the dark web...

contentBriefs.title: Wild Pokédex AI Twist
contentBriefs.summary: Wild Pokédex combines Pokémon with AI. Create realistic Pokémon scenarios using AI generation.
contentBriefs.hook: What if Pokémon were real? I used AI to find out

contentBriefs.title: Triple Threat Viral Video - AI Creature Evolution
contentBriefs.summary: Synthesize all three proven viral patterns: time compression, thief-bait with educational payoff, and escalating stakes comparison. The '1000 Days' format with AI creature evolution using tiered tool investment.
contentBriefs.hook: I Survived 100 Days with a $10,000 AI Creature (It Escaped)
outline: Hook (0-30s): Thief-bait opening - creature has 'escaped' the simulation
Setup (30s-2min): Explain challenge - 100 days of evolution with escalating tool investment
Time Compression (2-10min): Day-by-day evolution highlights with clear progression
Price Comparison Integration: Tool upgrades at day 25, 50, 75, 100
Payoff (12-15min): Educational breakdown of what made the creature 'successful'

trendAnalysis.instructions: Use viewstats.com Pro Tools > Outlier to research each competitor channel. Look for videos with outlier scores >2.0x. Document: title, views, outlier score, why it performed well, content angle for your channel.
targetPatterns: I got a fictional pet simulation

synthesizedInsights.evidence: borocg's 'What Makes A Great Monster Design?' (1.26M views, 99.1 outlier) + drawsessions tutorials (631K views)
synthesizedInsights.finding: Creature design educational content significantly outperforms pure entertainment in this niche
synthesizedInsights.actionable: All AI creature content should include educational breakdown of design principles

synthesizedInsights.evidence: boxofficemovies4k creature horror film (2.18M views, 103 outlier)
synthesizedInsights.finding: Long-form creature content (20+ min) has strong audience retention when narrative-driven
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Triple Threat format should target 15-18 min, not short-form

synthesizedInsights.evidence: Nat Geo Desert Creatures (3.36M views) consistently performs despite lower outlier score
synthesizedInsights.finding: Nature documentary narration style builds trust and perceived value
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Use documentary-style voiceover for AI creature reveals - 'National Geographic of the AI world'

synthesizedInsights.pattern: Time Compression + Creature Evolution = Viral Formula
synthesizedInsights.evidence: ZMDE's '1000 Years' series consistently achieves 40-50x subscriber view ratios. Pattern repeatable across 20+ videos with different creatures (dragons, sharks, Pokemon, etc).
synthesizedInsights.finding: Time compression creates built-in retention hooks. Each 'year' milestone gives viewers dopamine hit. Creature evolution provides visual progression that satisfies completion bias.
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Create 'I Evolved an AI Creature for 100 Generations' series. Each video = different creature type. 10-gen milestones with dramatic visual changes.

synthesizedInsights.pattern: Tutorial Content Solving Specific Problems = Extreme Outliers
synthesizedInsights.evidence: evanlandchannel's Pet Sim transfer guide (471x) - specific utility content outperforms general entertainment
synthesizedInsights.finding: How-to content addressing specific game mechanics or platform transfers achieves massive outlier scores in gaming niche
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Create 'How to X with your AI pet' tutorials - specific utility drives shares and bookmarks

synthesizedInsights.evidence: sinpoke's 'What if Pokemon had more Evolution Stages?' (676x) - highest outlier score found
synthesizedInsights.finding: 'What if X had more Y' speculation format triggers imagination and drives massive engagement. Viewers love imagining beyond established canon.
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Apply to AI creatures: 'What if [creature] had 10 evolution stages?' or 'What if [creature] evolved backwards?'

synthesizedInsights.evidence: Multiple high-performing outliers using 'All X' format: ALL MINECRAFT MOBS vs BOSS MOBS (49.9x), ALL AQUATIC CREATURES (14.2x), Every Type Of Zombie Ever (6.5x), All Pacific Rim Kaiju Evolution (10.5x)
synthesizedInsights.finding: Viewers have strong completionist desire - wanting to see comprehensive collections of creature types. 'All X' promises complete information in one video.
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Create 'All AI [Creature Type]' series - comprehensive catalogs of AI creature variations. Episode ideas: 'All AI Dragons,' 'All AI Sea Creatures,' 'All AI Mythical Beasts'

synthesizedInsights.pattern: Physics Simulation + Miniature Creatures = Extreme Engagement
synthesizedInsights.evidence: steve_big_guns '2d physics baby kaiju' (390x outlier, 226K views on 2.1K subs). foxieplaysblox 'ZOOCHOSIS' mod showcase (677x outlier, 3.37M views on 51K subs).
synthesizedInsights.finding: Physics-based creature interactions create satisfying visual feedback loops. 'Baby' or miniature versions of creatures trigger protective/cute responses. Modded sandbox games (Garry's Mod) enable physics demonstrations without engine development.
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Create 'Baby AI [Creature] Physics Simulation' series. Use simple physics engines or sandbox games to show AI creatures interacting with realistic physics. Focus on cute-but-destructive contrast.

synthesizedInsights.evidence: catloversforum_en 'How a cat works' (75.3x outlier, 3.84M views on 51K subs). cannymem 'How a dog works' (5.4x). sxuiroblox 'How To Make A Pet System' (3.1x). zedwinyt 'How To Get EVERY HUGE PET' (66.7x).
synthesizedInsights.finding: Simple 'How X Works' educational format achieves massive outlier scores, especially with pets/creatures. The cat video at 75x is one of the highest outliers found. Tutorial-style content solving specific problems (66.7x) combines education with utility.
synthesizedInsights.actionable: Create 'How [AI Creature] Works' series - explain fictional biology, behavior, and mechanics in documentary style. Include 'How To' guides for specific AI creature interactions and mechanics.

addedAt.evidence: cozmouz multiple high-performers: 'I Trapped this AI Centipede' (2.27M views), 'I Trapped this AI Worm' (2.45M views), 'I Trapped this AI Bot in Parkour' (259K views), 'AI Learns to Outrun Police' (746K views). All use 'I trapped X in simulation' format.
addedAt.finding: The 'trapped in simulation' narrative creates built-in tension and progression. Viewers want to see if AI escapes or adapts. Combining with parkour/chase mechanics adds dynamic movement. Police outrun variant adds antagonist dynamic.
addedAt.actionable: Create 'I Trapped AI [Creature] in [Simulation Type]' series. Variations: escape rooms, mazes, obstacle courses, survival scenarios. Show AI learning/adapting over time. Include cliffhangers: 'Will it escape? Find out in Part 2!'

steps: 4. Set date range to last 90 days
